location.origin: Chewelah, Washington -- Stevens County, northeast Washington, approximately 300 miles east of Seattle across the Cascade Range; population ~2,600; father Danny Stone was minister at Addy New Life Christian Center; mother was an OB/GYN nurse; first artist from outside the Seattle metro area in twenty-four degrees
location.formative: Chewelah church (age 3-18, gospel worship leading, guitar); bedroom self-education in soul/R&B from recordings (Stevie Wonder Innervisions at age 15, then Marvin Gaye, Donny Hathaway, Bill Withers, Sam Cooke); Stacie Orrico peer inspiration; no formal music education, no conservatory, no music scene access
location.career: Seattle (roughly 2008-2017) -- small venue circuit (Crocodile, Neumos, Triple Door, Nectar Lounge, Neptune Theatre, Paramount Theatre); viral YouTube video 'Unaware' from mother's living room in Chewelah (April 2011, 16M+ views); national late-night television (Conan, Letterman, Leno); ATO Records signing (2012); Capitol Records detour (2015, Radius); return to ATO; national touring (70 shows in 2024); relocated to Spokane, WA (~2017); Berklee Herb Alpert Visiting Artist (2023)

members.name: Allen Stone
members.note: Born in Chewelah, WA; son of a preacher; began singing in church at age 3; self-taught soul vocalist through recordings; 'pitch-perfect powerhouse' (USA Today); compared to Stevie Wonder, Marvin Gaye, Donny Hathaway, Bill Withers by The New York Times; named Berklee Herb Alpert Visiting Artist 2023

theory_nodes.concept: Gospel-to-soul vocal pipeline without institutional mediation (the vocal technique developed through church worship -- melisma, dynamic range, falsetto, call-and-response -- channeled directly into soul music without conservatory training, jazz detour, or formal education; the most direct gospel-to-soul connection in the engine; church as conservatory-equivalent with different content)
examples: Allen Stone (degree 23) -- church worship leader at age 3 in Chewelah, WA; self-taught soul from recordings at age 15; no conservatory, no jazz training; gospel technique applied directly to soul performance; the unmediated pipeline
Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) -- church-trained vocalist channeled through jazz tradition; gospel roots mediated by jazz (comparison -- mediated vs. unmediated pipeline)
Maktub (degree 18) -- Reggie Watts' vocal training at Cornish filtered through soul; formal training mediated by institution (comparison -- institutional vs. autodidactic)
Sam Cooke (external reference) -- the paradigmatic gospel-to-soul transition; from Soul Stirrers to solo career; the historical model Stone's trajectory echoes
Aretha Franklin (external reference) -- church-trained voice channeled through R&B/soul; the gold standard of the gospel-to-soul pipeline
theory_nodes.description: The gospel-to-soul vocal pipeline without institutional mediation describes a vocal technique development path where church worship singing (melisma, dynamic range from whisper to shout, falsetto as emotional texture, call-and-response audience engagement) is channeled directly into soul music performance without the intermediary step of formal musical education, conservatory training, or jazz tradition immersion. Allen Stone's path -- church singing from age 3, discovery of soul recordings at age 15, no formal training, public performance of soul music -- is the purest form of this pipeline in the engine. The church provides the two things a conservatory provides (vocal technique through thousands of hours of practice, and audience comfort through worship leading) but not theory, professional networks, or genre context. The soul language is acquired separately, from recordings, in isolation. This makes the pipeline functionally autodidactic: the institution (church) provides the instrument (trained voice); the recordings provide the language (soul); the artist connects them without external guidance.

ancestors: Gospel worship tradition -- the vocal technique source: melisma, dynamic range, falsetto, call-and-response, emotional directness
Stevie Wonder Innervisions (1973) -- the specific recording that triggered Stone's soul education at age 15
Sam Cooke gospel-to-soul transition -- the historical paradigm: Soul Stirrers to solo career, sacred to secular, same voice different context
Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) -- church-trained vocalist in jazz context; gospel roots mediated by genre (comparison: mediated)
Maktub (degree 18) -- Cornish-trained vocalist in soul context; institutional roots mediated by education (comparison: institutional)
genealogy.relationship: The unmediated gospel-to-soul pipeline is the most direct version of a phenomenon that has appeared in mediated form throughout the engine. Anderson (3) mediated gospel through jazz. Watts/Maktub (18) mediated formal training through soul. Stone removes the intermediary: gospel technique applied directly to soul, with the soul vocabulary acquired autodidactically from recordings. This produces a different vocal character -- rawer, more emotionally direct, less harmonically sophisticated than jazz-mediated gospel, but more connected to the original worship function of the voice.

theory_nodes.concept: Internet-mediated career ignition (the first career in the engine launched by a viral video rather than by live performance reputation, institutional connections, or label investment; the YouTube video as democratic talent discovery mechanism; 'Unaware' from mother's living room as the structural pivot from local to national; the medium changes the velocity of career development)
examples: Allen Stone (degree 23) -- 'Unaware' live from his mother's living room (April 2011), 16M+ YouTube views, led to Conan/Letterman/Leno appearances and ATO Records deal; the video IS the career ignition event

Foxy Apollo (degree 22) -- internet-era band using Bandcamp and streaming for distribution, but career built through live performance first; internet as distribution, not ignition (comparison -- distribution vs. ignition)
Reggie Watts (degree 17) -- YouTube presence as career amplifier after Comedy Bang Bang established him; internet as amplifier for existing reputation (comparison -- amplifier vs. ignition)
Every prior degree (0-22) -- careers built through live performance, institutional reputation, or label investment; pre-internet or internet-as-supplement model
theory_nodes.description: Internet-mediated career ignition is the phenomenon where a single piece of online content (video, recording, social media post) becomes the primary catalyst for a music career's transition from local to national/international visibility. Unlike internet-as-distribution (Foxy Apollo using Bandcamp) or internet-as-amplifier (using YouTube to extend an existing reputation), internet-mediated ignition means the online content IS the event that launches the career -- without it, the career trajectory would be fundamentally different. Allen Stone's 'Unaware' video is the engine's first clear instance: filmed in a modest living room in rural eastern Washington, it accumulated 16 million views and directly generated late-night television offers that transformed a small-venue Seattle singer into a nationally touring artist. The structural implication is that the internet democratizes talent discovery -- Stone did not need to be in New York, did not need a label, did not need a booking agent. The video did the work that institutions and geography used to do.

ancestors: YouTube as talent discovery platform -- the technological infrastructure that made viral music videos possible
Living room recording as authenticity signal -- the modest setting contrasting with vocal power creates narrative tension audiences find compelling
Late-night television as career amplifier -- Conan, Letterman, Leno as the post-viral institutional gatekeepers who converted online attention into industry legitimacy
Allen Stone's self-released first album Last to Speak (2010) -- the pre-viral career that the video ignited; the talent existed before the medium discovered it
genealogy.relationship: Internet-mediated career ignition is a post-2005 phenomenon that the engine documents for the first time at degree 23. All prior degrees built careers through pre-internet or internet-supplemented mechanisms. Stone's career represents the inflection point: born 1987, career ignited 2011, in the first generation where YouTube could serve as a talent discovery platform equivalent to (or more powerful than) traditional institutional pathways.

theory_nodes.concept: The independent label as career amplifier (ATO Records model: artist-first, career-building, creative control, master rights retention; label amplifies artist's existing creative direction rather than directing it; contrasted with major label model where label directs creative output and optimizes for hits; Stone's Capitol-to-ATO trajectory as structural proof; first active label in twenty-four degrees)
examples: Allen Stone on ATO Records (degree 23) -- ATO re-released the self-titled album (2012), then released Building Balance (2019) and APART (2021); label amplified Stone's existing artistic direction without redirecting it

Allen Stone on Capitol Records (degree 23, Radius 2015) -- major label demanded hits, rejected authentic recordings, pushed pop-oriented material; Stone asked to be dropped; label-as-director model (comparison -- director vs. amplifier)
Foxy Apollo (degree 22) -- self-released, no label infrastructure; pure DIY model (comparison -- no label vs. independent label)
ATO Records roster (external) -- Brittany Howard, Black Pumas, Alabama Shakes, My Morning Jacket, Brandi Carlile; pattern of artist-first career-building across the roster
theory_nodes.description: The independent label as career amplifier describes a label-artist relationship where the label provides distribution, marketing, and infrastructure support while the artist retains creative control and, where possible, master rights. The label amplifies what the artist already is rather than reshaping the artist into what the label thinks the market wants. ATO Records (founded 2000 by Dave Matthews and Coran Capshaw) exemplifies this model. Allen Stone's career provides the direct comparison: Capitol Records (major, 2015) demanded creative compromise, rejected authentic recordings, pushed for pop hits; ATO Records (independent, 2012-present) released Stone's music as he intended it. Stone asked Capitol to drop him and returned to ATO -- the strongest possible endorsement of the independent-amplifier model over the major-director model. This is the first active label in twenty-four degrees of the engine, and its structural position (amplifier, not director) sets the baseline for all future label analysis.

ancestors: ATO Records founding (2000) -- Dave Matthews and Coran Capshaw creating an artist-first independent label; David Gray's White Ladder as first success
Self-release tradition (degrees 18-22) -- the five consecutive self-released degrees that precede the first label entry; the baseline of total creative control against which label models are measured
Capitol Records major-label model -- the counter-example that proves the independent model's value; A&R turnover, creative compromise demands, Stone asking to be dropped
Independent label philosophy -- career-building over hit-seeking, master rights retention, long-term artist development
genealogy.relationship: The independent label as career amplifier sits between two poles the engine has already documented: self-release (total control, no amplification, degrees 18-22) and no label infrastructure (career predates modern label system, degrees 0-12). ATO at degree 23 introduces the middle position: the label that adds value without subtracting autonomy. Stone's Capitol detour documents the opposite pole (major label as creative director) and confirms that soul music specifically benefits from the independent model because the genre's authenticity is incompatible with hit-oriented creative direction.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- gospel-vocal (twenty-third deployment; vocal bending between minor and major thirds derived from gospel worship tradition rather than jazz tradition; the gospel blue note is emotionally expressive rather than harmonically exploratory; melismatic bending as worship technique applied to soul performance; the most direct church-to-stage blue note in the engine)
examples: Allen Stone (degree 23) -- gospel-trained vocal bending applied to soul music; melismatic blue notes inherited from church worship; emotionally expressive rather than harmonically exploratory; the unmediated gospel blue note
Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) -- jazz-vocal blue note; church roots channeled through jazz vocabulary (comparison -- jazz-mediated vs. direct gospel)
Maktub (degree 18) -- soul-vocal blue note; Cornish-trained vocalist in soul context (comparison -- institutional vs. autodidactic)
Foxy Apollo (degree 22) -- vocal-funk blue note; pop-funk vocal bending (comparison -- funk context vs. soul context)
Sam Cooke (external reference) -- the paradigmatic gospel blue note converted to secular soul; 'A Change Is Gonna Come' as the canonical gospel-to-soul vocal bend
theory_nodes.description: Twenty-third deployment of the blue note thread. The gospel-vocal blue note is the vocal bending technique inherited directly from church worship singing -- specifically, the melismatic movement between minor and major thirds that gospel singers use to convey emotional intensity. This differs from the jazz blue note (which creates harmonic tension and ambiguity) and the funk blue note (which creates rhythmic emphasis). The gospel blue note is EXPRESSIVE: it conveys feeling directly, without the intellectual mediation of jazz harmony. Allen Stone's blue note is the most direct church-to-stage deployment in the engine because his vocal training is entirely church-derived, with no conservatory or jazz mediation. Twenty-three mechanisms now documented across twenty-four degrees.

ancestors: Blue note -- all twenty-two prior deployments (degrees 0-22)
Blue note -- vocal (degree 3, Anderson); church-trained voice in jazz context
Blue note -- soul-vocal (degree 18, Maktub); Cornish-trained voice in soul context
Blue note -- vocal-funk (degree 22, Foxy Apollo); pop-funk vocal bending
Gospel worship melisma tradition -- the original source of Stone's vocal bending technique
genealogy.relationship: Twenty-three deployments across twenty-four degrees. The gospel-vocal deployment returns the blue note to its most ancestral form -- church worship singing -- bypassing all the mediation (jazz, funk, post-genre) that prior vocal deployments have applied. Twenty-three mechanisms: bass-line (0), guitar (1), vocal (3), trumpet-warm (4), piano crush (5), altered voicings (6), trombone slide (7), bass fundamental (8), sax voice (9), alto cool bend (10), flute bent (11), bebop chromatic (12), modal integration (13), post-genre floating (14), assertive brass (15), synthesizer pitch bend (16), vocal glide (17), soul-vocal (18), collective-soul (19), americana-soul (20), funk-brass (21), vocal-funk (22), gospel-vocal (23).

seattle_connection: Soul/R&B singer born in Chewelah, WA (pop ~2,600), 300 miles east of Seattle. FIRST RURAL ORIGIN in the engine -- every prior artist originated in or near Seattle or another major city. Moved to Seattle after high school, built career through small-venue circuit (Crocodile, Neumos, Triple Door, Neptune Theatre). Viral YouTube video 'Unaware' from mother's living room in Chewelah (16M+ views) launched national career. Signed to ATO Records (2012) -- FIRST ACTIVE LABEL in twenty-four degrees. Capitol Records detour (2015, Radius) -- asked to be dropped, returned to ATO. BERKLEE VISITING ARTIST (2023) -- connects to Jones (0) and Frisell (1) Berklee pipeline. Relocated to Spokane, WA (~2017) -- BOOMERANG trajectory, first artist to return to eastern WA. THE TESTIMONY as twenty-fourth acoustic element -- gospel-derived vocal witness. THE RURAL DRAW as twenty-fourth artist-city pattern. THIRD CONSECUTIVE E=4 (distribution 2:13:6:3). Soul/Jazz returns (same genre as degrees 18-19). Gospel-to-soul pipeline without institutional mediation. Blue-eyed soul -- first degree where race-genre tension is explicitly documented. APART (2021) all-acoustic album as voice-isolating document. USA Today: 'pitch-perfect powerhouse.' NYT compared to Wonder, Gaye, Hathaway, Withers.

theory_genealogy: Gospel-to-soul vocal pipeline without institutional mediation (church worship technique channeled directly to soul without conservatory, jazz, or formal training; church as conservatory-equivalent; the most direct gospel-to-soul connection in the engine; three pipeline nodes now: Anderson 3 mediated-jazz, Maktub 18 institutional-soul, Stone 23 unmediated-soul)

Internet-mediated career ignition (first career launched by viral video rather than live reputation, institution, or label; 'Unaware' from mother's living room as structural pivot; the medium changes velocity; YouTube as democratic talent discovery; 16M+ views)

Independent label as career amplifier (ATO model: artist-first, career-building, creative control; contrasted with Capitol major-label-as-director; first active label in twenty-four degrees; Stone's Capitol-to-ATO trajectory as proof; soul music benefits from independent model)

Blue note -- twenty-third deployment; gospel-vocal (vocal bending from church worship tradition; emotionally expressive rather than harmonically exploratory; the most direct church-to-stage blue note; melismatic bending as worship technique applied to soul)

key_albums.title: Last to Speak
key_albums.label: Self-released
key_albums.significance: Debut album. Self-released in Seattle. Raw talent with limited production. The calling card of a singer still finding his professional footing. Pre-viral, pre-label.

key_albums.title: Allen Stone
key_albums.label: Self-released / ATO Records
key_albums.significance: Self-titled breakthrough album. Features 'Unaware' (16M+ YouTube views from mother's living room video). Backed by Raphael Saadiq's rhythm section and Deron Johnson (Miles Davis' keyboardist). #2 iTunes R&B/Soul, #9 Billboard Heatseekers, #35 Billboard R&B/Hip-Hop. ATO re-release marked label signing. The album that converted viral attention into a career.

key_albums.title: Radius
key_albums.label: Capitol Records
key_albums.significance: Capitol Records debut. #80 Billboard 200. The major-label experience Stone describes as creatively compromised: A&R team departed, original recordings rejected, forced to write with outside songwriters. Stone asked Capitol to drop him. Re-released on ATO in its intended form. The cautionary tale.

key_albums.title: Building Balance
key_albums.label: ATO Records
key_albums.significance: Return to ATO. Mix of 1970s soul and 1990s New Jack Swing sounds. Compared to D'Angelo and Stevie Wonder by critics. Full artistic control restored. The post-Capitol creative recovery.

key_albums.title: APART
key_albums.label: ATO Records
key_albums.significance: First all-acoustic album. Revisits catalog favorites in stripped-down arrangements. The most honest document of Stone's voice without production, band, or studio machinery. The voice itself, isolated.

key_albums.title: Echoes of Harmony
key_albums.label: ATO Records
key_albums.significance: Most recent album. Features 'Back to the Roots' single. Mix of soul, R&B, and folk with themes of unity, individuality, connection. Released from Spokane home base. The settled-phase album.

degree_neighbors.name: Maktub
degree_neighbors.relationship: Soul/Jazz genre match. Both voice-centered, emotionally direct, groove-supported. Different vocal pipelines: Maktub's Watts Cornish-trained; Stone church-trained autodidact. Different Seattle relationships: Maktub formed in Seattle; Stone arrived from rural WA. Maktub E=3 vs. Stone E=4 -- energy escalation within Soul/Jazz.

degree_neighbors.name: Fly Moon Royale
degree_neighbors.relationship: Soul/Jazz genre match (second pairing). Both collective enterprises centered on primary vocalist. FMR rotating membership vs. Stone's consistent touring band. Both Soul/Jazz but different jazz relationships.

degree_neighbors.name: Foxy Apollo
degree_neighbors.relationship: Immediate degree neighbor. Both E=4. Completely different origins: Foxy Apollo inherited Seattle scene (The Inheritor); Stone arrived from rural WA (The Rural Draw). Jazz/Funk (22) to Soul/Jazz (23) = genre shift. Both post-internet generation.

degree_neighbors.name: Polyrhythmics
degree_neighbors.relationship: E=4 streak (third consecutive). Three subtypes: ensemble (21), collective (22), vocal (23). Instrumental funk vs. vocal soul -- groove-voice polarity.

degree_neighbors.name: Ernestine Anderson
degree_neighbors.relationship: Gospel-to-performance pipeline. Both church-trained vocalists. Anderson through jazz; Stone through soul. Different eras (1940s vs. 2010s), different genres, same source (church).

degree_neighbors.name: Quincy Jones
degree_neighbors.relationship: Berklee connection. Jones attended (1951); Stone named 2023 Visiting Artist. Autodidact invited to teach at prodigy's institution.

degree_neighbors.name: Don Lanphere
degree_neighbors.relationship: Eastern Washington connection. Lanphere from Wenatchee; Stone from Chewelah. Both crossed the Cascades. Lanphere's Prodigal pattern prefigures Stone's Rural Draw/Boomerang.

degree_neighbors.name: Bill Frisell
degree_neighbors.relationship: Berklee pipeline and guitar connection. Frisell Berklee graduate; Stone Berklee visiting artist. Frisell guitar-as-texture (1); Stone guitar-as-rhythm/accompaniment (23).

degree_neighbors.name: Jeff Lorber
degree_neighbors.relationship: Berklee pipeline. Lorber Berklee graduate (16); Stone Berklee visiting artist (23). Four Berklee connections in twenty-four degrees.

garfield_thread.degree_3: Ernestine Anderson -- Garfield, same era as Jones
garfield_thread.degree_7: Julian Priester -- NOT Garfield. DuSable (Chicago). Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_9: Hadley Caliman -- NOT Garfield. LA jazz scene. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_17: Reggie Watts -- NOT Garfield. Great Falls, MT. Cornish College of the Arts student.
garfield_thread.degree_21: Grant Schroff (Polyrhythmics) -- NOT Garfield. Cornish graduate.
garfield_thread.degree_22: Sam Ashkenazy (Foxy Apollo) -- NOT Garfield. NOT Cornish. High school jazz band (unspecified).
garfield_thread.degree_23: Allen Stone -- NOT Garfield. NOT Cornish. NOT UW. Church worship leader in rural eastern WA. Berklee College of Music Herb Alpert Visiting Artist (2023). Second consecutive non-institutional entry, but different alternative pathway: church training + autodidactic record study (vs. Foxy Apollo's scene inheritance).

cultural_patterns.institution_type: the rural draw -- artist drawn to Seattle from a rural origin within Washington State (Chewelah, pop ~2,600, Stevens County, northeast WA); rural-to-urban migration for music career; BOOMERANG trajectory: arrived Seattle (~2008), built career, returned to eastern WA (Spokane, ~2017); career decoupled from city residency via internet and touring; church worship as conservatory-equivalent; first non-metro origin in twenty-four degrees
cultural_patterns.gender: male solo artist (gender gap continues at 3:21 women:men/male-led)
cultural_patterns.gender_barrier: CONTINUES -- male solo artist; gender gap moves to 3:21 (women:men/male-led); second consecutive male solo-led entry; twenty-first consecutive male/male-led degree since Bown (6), broken only by Monge (20)

duwamish_acknowledgment: Inherited from degree 20. Allen Stone's career was built in Seattle on Duwamish ancestral territory. His origin in Chewelah, WA (Stevens County) falls within the broader Inland Salish world including Kalispel, Spokane, and Colville Confederated Tribes territory. All releases inherit the Duwamish territorial acknowledgment established at degree 20.
